name: Anthropic Distillation Campaigns - Constrained ATT&CK Analogies
description: Analyst-created ATT&CK crosswalk for provider-reported API distillation campaigns. Entries are analogies with confidence caveats, not official MITRE or Anthropic mappings. See the report's MITRE ATLAS crosswalk for AI-native behavior.

techniques.comment: MEDIUM confidence analogy. Anthropic reported use of commercial proxy services. ATT&CK normally frames acquired web services as operational infrastructure, often for C2 or exfiltration.

HIGH confidence analogy. Anthropic reported large-scale creation of service accounts. Cloud Account is semantically appropriate for SaaS account creation; the previous Local Account mapping was incorrect.
MEDIUM confidence analogy. Requests used functioning accounts and API credentials, but public evidence describes fraudulent enrollment rather than compromise of an existing victim cloud tenant.
LOW confidence analogy. Commercial proxies reportedly obscured origin and coordinated access. ATT&CK defines External Proxy in a C2 context, which was not publicly demonstrated here.
LOW confidence analogy. The reported campaign automated output retrieval, but no compromised enterprise environment or conventional exfiltration path was publicly demonstrated.
